summary.name: Garmuti
key_uses: Expert Gastrointestinal and Diarrhea Care Specialist (Grahi)

plant_profile.history: Unique History: A tremendously hardy, drought-resistant wild bean recognized by ancient pastoralists as a life-saving 'fever-breaking' food during extreme heat and drought. Physical Characteristics: A trailing or twining, deeply rooted annual or perennial herb with deeply three-lobed ('trilobata') leaves. Flowers are small and yellow. The pods are slender, cylindrical, containing tiny, black or mottled brown seeds. Found wild in arid, rocky soils and pastures of India. Specific Chemical Compounds: Contains Quercetin, Kaempferol, and diverse Saponins. These form its anti-oxidative, cooling, and liver-protecting base. Strict Standardizations: Standardized based on its deeply three-lobed leaves and tiny, hard seeds.

ayurvedic_properties.dosha_modulation: Prominently pacifies Pitta and Kapha doshas. Due to its Madhura (sweet) and Tikta (bitter) Rasa, and Sheeta (cooling) Virya, it is a highly specialized wild legume for 'Raktaprasadana' (blood-soothing) and 'Jwarahara' (fever-reducing) therapies. It is 'Laghu' (light) and 'Ruksha' (dry), making it a mild Vata-increasing herb.

medicinal_uses.expert_gastrointestinal_and_diarrhea_car: Garmuti is the premier wild pulse for managing burning diarrhea and dysentery in arid regions. Its 'Grahi' (absorbent) and 'Kashaya' (astringent) actions help in strengthening the gut lining.
medicinal_uses.fever_and_heat_management__jwarahara_: Provides instant relief from internal burning sensations (Daha), sunstrokes, and inflammatory fevers through its high 'Sheeta' (cooling) and 'Tikta' (bitter) properties.
medicinal_uses.internal_hemorrhage_and_bleeding_special: Effectively cools the blood and stops internal bleeding from the lungs, stomach, and pelvic organs by providing systemic cooling.
medicinal_uses.hepatoprotective_and_anti_toxic: Historically known as 'Vishaghna' (anti-toxic). It effectively reduces hepatic heat, clears jaundice markers, and removes metabolic toxins from the blood.
medicinal_uses.phytochemical_profile: Rich in Proteins (18-20%), Dietary Fiber, Phenolic acids (Gallic acid, Ferulic acid), Flavonoids (Quercetin, Kaempferol). The flavonoids act as intense hepato-protective and anti-diarrheal agents.

preparation_methods.garmuti_yusha__medicated_soup_: 1 part seeds boiled with 14 parts water, seasoned with ginger and cumin. Take as a primary meal for managing diarrhea and liver heat.
preparation_methods.kashaya__decoction_: Whole plant or seeds boiled and reduced. Taken for severe fever and jaundice.
preparation_methods.leaf_paste__lepa_: Fresh leaves crushed and applied to snake-bites, insect stings, or inflammatory swellings.
preparation_methods.seed_powder__churna_: 3-6g of fine powder taken with buttermilk.

dosage.adults: 10-20g of pulse cooked as soup, or 3-6g of powder per day.
dosage.children: Generally safe. 1-2 grams of powder mixed with honey once twice for diarrhea.
dosage.elderly: Highly recommended for Pitta-dominant diarrhea and weakness.

contraindications: Generally very safe. However, individuals with high Vata (severe constipation, bloating, dry skin) should use it in moderation and always cook with digestive spices like ginger and hing.
side_effects: Overconsumption leads to excessive gas and bloating if not appropriately toasted before cooking.
drug_interactions: None documented. It acts as a neutral carrier for many medicines.
safety.toxicity_notes: GRAS Status: Classified as a safe medicinal wild food.
